description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a symmetrical, crescent-shaped blade on each side, both painted or coated in a matte black finish. The axe features a long, straight handle that appears to be made of wood with a dark, possibly metal or painted grip section adorned with a series of rounded, bead-like segments for enhanced grip. The handle ends in a rounded pommel, also dark in color, which may serve as a counterweight or for striking. The transition between the blade and the handle is reinforced, suggesting durability and strength for heavy-duty use. The overall design is both functional and visually striking, with a blend of traditional and modern elements. This axe would be suitable for display, theatrical use, or as a prop in urban-themed scenarios.
description: A double-headed black battle axe with a long, beaded handle and symmetrical crescent blades. The handle combines wood and dark grip segments, ending in a rounded pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, symmetrical crescent blades and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the rounded, segmented handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, identical to the front, displaying both crescent blades and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and handle details.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical handle with a beaded grip section, primarily wood with dark painted or metal grip, rounded pommel.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical crescent blades with wide cutting edges, no beard or poll, matte black finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with reinforced transition between blade and handle.
